name: Sierra de Almenara and Cabo Cope Loop
name_original: Lomo de Bas y Alto de Tebar — vuelta por el Parque Regional Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre
description: This moderate 32.9-mile road cycling loop through Sierra de Almenara and Cabo Cope offers diverse scenery and coastal views.
region_intro: Road cycling around Águilas offers diverse terrain, from undulating coastal routes with sea views to challenging inland rides through arid plains and forested valleys. The region features a varied topography, including the Sierra de Almenara and the protected Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre Natural Park. Cyclists can find routes that hug the Mediterranean coastline or venture into more demanding areas with significant elevation changes. The area provides a range of road cycling experiences suitable for different skill levels. Best road cycling routes around Águilas The most popular road cycling route is Climb of the Cuesta de Gos from Playa de la Cola — return via the Sierra de Mayorales, a 28.1 miles (45.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 22 minutes to complete. This difficult route features a significant climb and traverses the Sierra de Mayorales. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de Almenara and Cabo Cope Loop, a moderate 32.9 miles (53.0 km) path. This route leads through the Sierra de Almenara and the Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre Natural Park, offering varied landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the Coastal Path With Sea Views – Cala Cristal Views loop from Águilas-El Labradorcico, a 41.4 miles (66.6 km) trail leading through coastal areas with continuous sea views, often completed in about 2 hours 51 minutes. Road cycling around Águilas is defined by undulating coastal paths, arid plains, and forested valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate coastal rides to challenging inland ascents. The routes in Águilas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 50 reviews. More than 2,800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Águilas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Águilas?
A: Águilas offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with nearly 70 different tours available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 9 easy, 32 moderate, and 28 difficult options, ensuring there's a ride for every cyclist.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Águilas?
A: Águilas, part of Spain's 'Costa Cálida,' boasts a Mediterranean subtropical climate with approximately 320 days of sunshine annually. Temperatures typically range between 17 and 32 degrees Celsius, creating optimal conditions for road cycling throughout much of the year, especially outside the peak summer heat.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Águilas?
A: The region offers a highly diverse topography. You can enjoy undulating coastal routes with continuous sea views, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33935801" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal Path With Sea Views – Cala Cristal Views loop from Águilas-El Labradorcico&lt;/a&gt;, or venture inland for more demanding rides through arid plains and forested valleys. Expect well-maintained roads and generally low traffic, making for a pleasant and safe cycling experience.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Águilas?
A: Yes, Águilas provides several challenging routes for experienced cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009058503/climb-of-the-cuesta-de-gos-from-playa-de-la-cola-return-via-the-sierra-de-mayorales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb of the Cuesta de Gos from Playa de la Cola — return via the Sierra de Mayorales&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 45.3 km route with significant elevation gain. Other demanding options include rides through the Sierra de Almenara and routes that ascend the Alto del Campo López, which can climb to over a thousand meters.

Q: Can I find routes with significant elevation gain in Águilas?
A: Absolutely. While coastal routes offer gentler profiles, many inland tours feature substantial climbs. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009058503/climb-of-the-cuesta-de-gos-from-playa-de-la-cola-return-via-the-sierra-de-mayorales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb of the Cuesta de Gos from Playa de la Cola — return via the Sierra de Mayorales&lt;/a&gt; route includes over 700 meters of elevation gain, providing a good workout for those seeking ascents.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near significant natural features and landmarks. You can cycle through the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Almenara&lt;/a&gt; and the Cabo Cope y Puntas de Calnegre Natural Park. Coastal rides offer views of beautiful beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4085357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cocedores Beach&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4201747" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Higuerica Beach&lt;/a&gt;, as well as the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/535410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cope Tower&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, look out for viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6033937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Coast and Mountains near Cabo de Gata&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any loop road cycling routes in Águilas?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Águilas are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009073380/sierra-de-almenara-and-cabo-cope-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Almenara and Cabo Cope Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 53 km, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38297888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Delicias Beach – San Juan de los Terreros Beach loop from Águilas&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 45 km ride.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Águilas?
A: The road cycling experience in Águilas is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from scenic coastal paths with continuous sea views to challenging inland ascents, as well as the generally good road conditions and low traffic.

Q: Are there any routes that are particularly scenic or offer great viewpoints?
A: Águilas is renowned for its scenic beauty. Routes along the coast, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33917152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Cristal Views – Coastal Path With Sea Views loop from Águilas-El Labradorcico&lt;/a&gt;, offer spectacular Mediterranean vistas. Inland, routes through the Sierra de Almenara and near Cabo Cope provide impressive mountain and natural park scenery, with various viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3141198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cocón de Cope&lt;/a&gt; offering panoramic outlooks.

Q: Are there any specific mountain passes I can tackle on a road bike in Águilas?
A: For those looking to conquer mountain passes, Águilas offers several options. Notable passes in the area include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3579469" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de las Crucecicas&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5367638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lomo de Bas Pass&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5389275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de la Cuesta de Gos&lt;/a&gt;. These provide challenging climbs and rewarding views for road cyclists.

Q: Are the roads in Águilas suitable for road cycling?
A: Yes, the roads in Águilas are generally in very good condition and experience low traffic, which contributes to a pleasant and safe cycling experience. The town council actively promotes sports tourism, including cycling, ensuring a favorable environment for road cyclists.
tips: Located within the Cabo Cope and Puntas de Calnegre Regional Park, we have beautiful views of the sea rolling with the road bike. A paradise for lovers of this discipline.

Beautiful pass with breathtaking views, which is great to drive from both sides. Maximum 10%m on average more like 5-7%. The asphalt is not bad, but the (rapid) descent can, as is so often the case in Spain, become problematic because the roads are generally good but occasionally have bad potholes.
Beautiful views of the Cabezo de Ramonete where the remains of the Castle of Amir are found while we ride along the highway of the Murcia region 332. An ideal place to practice this sport.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling loop?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this 53 km (33 miles) road cycling loop is approximately 2 hours and 44 minutes. This can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and any stops you make to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain offers a diverse experience. In the Sierra de Almenara, you'll find beautiful roads with good climbs and generally well-paved surfaces, though some segments may be unpaved. The Cabo Cope section, part of a regional park, features less mountainous terrain, but it's crucial to note that some parts of a combined loop can include unpaved sections or 'desert paths' that are challenging for conventional road bikes. Careful route planning is advised to stick to paved roads.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate, requiring good fitness due to its 856 meters (2,808 feet) of elevation gain and potential for varied surfaces. While parts are well-paved, the presence of unpaved sections in both the Sierra de Almenara and Cabo Cope areas can make it challenging for beginners on road bikes. Experienced cyclists will find it more manageable.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience contrasting and beautiful scenery. The Sierra de Almenara offers panoramic views of cultivated fields, and in season, blooming almond orchards, with vistas of the Guadalentín Valley. The Cabo Cope section, a protected regional park, provides stunning coastal views with dramatic cliffs and secluded coves, offering a more maritime landscape. Both areas are rich in biodiversity.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route features several points of interest. You can enjoy the View of the Coast from El Garrobillo Road, pass through the Lomo de Bas Pass, and see the Cabezo de Ramonete.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to road cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: The region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions outside of the peak summer heat. Spring (March-May) and Autumn (September-November) are often ideal, providing comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery, such as almond orchards in bloom during spring. Winter can also be suitable, though days are shorter.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: While a specific trailhead parking lot isn't designated for this combined loop, you can typically find parking in towns or villages near the Sierra de Almenara or Cabo Cope areas, such as Águilas or Calabardina, and start your ride from there. Always check local parking regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route, especially within Parque Regional de Calnegre y Cabo Cope?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads within the Parque Regional de Calnegre y Cabo Cope. However, as it's a protected area, always adhere to local regulations regarding environmental protection and respect any signage you encounter.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling, which is generally not suitable for dogs. While dogs might be permitted in certain parts of the regional park on leash, bringing a dog along for a 53 km road bike loop with significant elevation gain and potential unpaved sections is not recommended.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this road cycling route partially follows sections of other known paths. You will encounter segments of the [CIMA MU04-1] Sierra de Almenara * Ermita del Ramonete, Camino Las Palomas, Sendero del Mediterráneo - Murcia, and Transmurciana 08.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for road cycling this loop?
faqs.answer: The main challenge for road cyclists is the potential for unpaved sections, particularly when combining routes through both Sierra de Almenara and Cabo Cope. An insider tip is to carefully plan your route using detailed maps or GPX files to ensure you stay on paved roads suitable for your racebike. Be prepared for good climbs in the Sierra de Almenara and enjoy the quiet roads with minimal traffic.

waypoints.name: View of the Coast from El Garrobillo Road
